nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## 2. Image Embedding Methods
[Read more about working with images in n8n]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

There are a [myriad of image embedding techniques]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} some which involve specialised models and some which do a simplified image-to-text representation.
In this demo, we'll use the simplified text representation methods: collecting color channel information and using Multimodal LLMs to produce keywords for the image. Together, these will form the document we'll embed to represent our image for search.

It is important to define your metadata for later filtering and retrieval purposes.

nodes.name: Sticky Note6

parameters.content:
## 3. Store in Vector Store
[Read more about vector stores]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Once our document is ready, we can just insert into any vector store to make it ready for searching. When searching, be sure to defined the same vector store index used here!
Note: Metadata is defined in the document loader which must be mapped manually.

nodes.name: Sticky Note8

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### This workflow does the following:
* Downloads a selected image from Google Drive.
* Extracts colour channel information from the image.
* Generates semantic keywords of the iamge using OpenAI vision model.
* Combines extracted and generated data to create an embedding document for the image.
* Inserts this document into a vector store to allow for vector search on the original image. 

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Get Image Keywords

parameters.text: Extract all possible semantic keywords which describe the image. Be comprehensive and be sure to identify subjects (if applicable) such as biological and non-biological objects, lightning, mood, tone, color, special effects, camera and/or techniques used if known. Respond with a comma-separated list.

This workflow integrates 12 different services: stickyNote, textSplitterRecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, vectorStoreInMemory, googleDrive, merge. It contains 26 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Manualtrigger Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.text: Extract all possible semantic keywords which describe the image. Be comprehensive and be sure to identify subjects (if applicable) such as biological and non-biological objects, lightning, mood, tone, color, special effects, camera and/or techniques used if known. Respond with a comma-separated list.

parameters.content: ⚠️ **Multimodal embedding is not designed analyze medical images for diagnostic features or disease patterns.** Please do not use Multimodal embedding for medical purposes.

## 1. Get the Source Image
In this demo, we just need an image file. We'll pull an image from google drive but you can use all input trigger or source you prefer.

## 2. Image Embedding Methods
[Read more about working with images in n8n]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

There are a [myriad of image embedding techniques]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} some which involve specialised models and some which do a simplified image-to-text representation.
In this demo, we'll use the simplified text representation methods: collecting color channel information and using Multimodal LLMs to produce keywords for the image. Together, these will form the document we'll embed to represent our image for search.

## 3. Generate Embedding Doc
It is important to define your metadata for later filtering and retrieval purposes.

## 3. Store in Vector Store
[Read more about vector stores]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Once our document is ready, we can just insert into any vector store to make it ready for searching. When searching, be sure to defined the same vector store index used here!
Note: Metadata is defined in the document loader which must be mapped manually.

## Try It Out!
### This workflow does the following:
* Downloads a selected image from Google Drive.
* Extracts colour channel information from the image.
* Generates semantic keywords of the iamge using OpenAI vision model.
* Combines extracted and generated data to create an embedding document for the image.
* Inserts this document into a vector store to allow for vector search on the original image. 

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Drive  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking "Test workflow"".
Open the "Google Drive" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Color Information" node (n8n-nodes-base.editImage) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Recursive Character Text Splitter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Combine Image Analysis" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Image Keywords" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "In-Memory Vector Store" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search for Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Document for Embedding".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
